page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-32 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 120 GB of RAM, providing a memory allocation of 3.75 GB per core. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Benchmark results indicate strong CPU and memory performance. The instance achieves top-tier results in text processing and small-model LLM text generation, reaching 411.9 tokens per second for a 135M parameter model. Single-threaded and multi-threaded decompression tasks via bzip3 show strong performance, alongside high static web serving throughput of 56.29 GB/sec. However, the lack of a dedicated GPU results in poor performance for large-scale LLM text generation, with the 70B model text generation dropping to 2.26 tokens per second. Memory bandwidth is measured at 147.21 GB/sec for large-block read operations.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-standard-32 offers a balanced resource density suitable for workloads that require dedicated compute power without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances. The inclusion of AMX and AVX-512 instructions allows it to handle lightweight machine learning inference and vector operations efficiently on the CPU. It is well-suited for workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, text processing, and database operations that benefit from a balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-32 is a general-purpose server instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 120 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with an x86_64 architecture. It provides 16 physical cores, a 260 MB L3 cache, and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs. Performance benchmarks show top-tier capabilities in text processing and small-model LLM text generation, alongside strong performance in single-threaded gzip compression and static web serving. However, it exhibits poor performance on large-scale LLM text generation due to the absence of GPU acceleration. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for balanced workloads, making it highly suitable for web serving, software compilation, text processing, and database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-32 is a general-purpose server featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs, 120 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. It is optimized for balanced workloads and text processing.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-32 features 32 dedicated vCPUs, 120 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor for balanced general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, advanced matrix extensions, and strong text processing capabilities.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs and 120 GB RAM powered by Intel Xeon 8581C
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated vector operations
Top-tier performance in text processing and small-model LLM text generation
Strong static web serving throughput and multi-threaded decompression
No local storage or GPU acceleration included
Best suited for web serving, software compilation, and database operations
